entities.name: upalabdhi
entities.cite: comes to be the condition for apprehension

axioms.name: avikara
axioms.cite: while the sabda is avikara, unchanging

axioms.name: vikrta
axioms.cite: The dhvani is vikrta nimittaih — modified by its causes

axioms.name: nimitta
axioms.cite: The sound's role is nimittatvam upalabdhau, being the condition of apprehension

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: the sound's modifications condition the apprehending (speed, clarity, audibility) without transferring change to the word apprehended

denials.name: parinamate
denials.cite: do not put flicker into the pot

denials.name: vikara
denials.cite: the sound's modifications condition the apprehending (speed, clarity, audibility) without transferring change to the word apprehended

rejected_readings.label: transfer_of_change
rejected_readings.rendering: The sound's modifications are transferred to the unchanging word, making it also modified.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly states that modifications condition apprehension without transferring change: 'do not put flicker into the pot.' This reading commits a kind error by asserting parinamate (real transformation) instead of vivarta (manifestation without transfer).

rejected_readings.label: shared_modification
rejected_readings.rendering: Both the sound and the word are modified, since the sound is the manifestation of the word.
rejected_readings.why: Conflates the sound's causal modifications (by speaker's organs, effort, pace) with the word's nature. The commentary's lamp analogy makes clear that the intermediary's changes do not affect what is mediated: the lamp's flicker does not put flicker in the pot.

notes: The verse establishes the manifestation (vivarta) structure: the sound (dhvani) is modified by external causes, yet serves as an uncontaminating condition for apprehending the unchanging word (sabda). The lamp analogy carries the proof: illumination mediates without corrupting. Strain: nimitta is modeled as both a power (the causal capacity) and a relation (the conditional link); this is resolvable if nimitta denotes the power-in-relation, but the six-sort system does not explicitly accommodate functional roles that span categories.
